design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (power meter depletion) against environmental hazards
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Guide a cherub through levels while managing energy/hunger state
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately rebranded licensed IP (Obake no Q-Tarō) into original Western property, suggesting design decisions driven by localization/publishing strategy rather than design innovation. Unusual protagonist (cherub) and mechanic (power meter management in platformer context).

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power meter depletion rate varies between regions (US version depletes slower than JP version), suggesting difficulty tuning through resource scarcity

replayability_hooks: High score tracking (regional variations in UI)
Power meter management challenges create variable playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Character redesign for regional localization (human-like characters redrawn for US version)
Title screen theming (dark US version vs. lively JP version)

boss_design.philosophy: Hazard-based (dog barks documented as lethal threat)
notable_bosses: Dogs/dog barks (primary antagonist hazard mentioned as cause of 'brutal' death)

environmental_storytelling: Power meter visual (Q logo in JP, hearts in US) indicates hunger/energy state

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Chubby Cherub article)
